title: Crunchy Smoky Potato Stir-fry
description: A full-fat sour cream-based dish with a smoky flavor and crunchy texture, this Norwegian-inspired recipe is jain-friendly and suitable for show-offs.
instructions: Peel and cut the potatoes into thin slices or coins.
In a pan, heat vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add potato slices and sauté until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes per side.
Add chopped garlic, chili flakes, salt, pepper, and soy sauce to the pan. Stir well.
Pour in tomato paste and continue stirring for another minute or so until it thickens slightly.
Mix in sour cream, and balsamic vinegar. Stir until all ingredients are combined evenly.
For a tadka variation, heat sesame oil over medium flame, add tadka seasoning, cumin seeds, mustard seeds, and curry leaves. Pour this mixture on the stir-fry potatoes and mix well.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for about 5 minutes to meld all flavors together.
